items.title: Smoking Cessation, Weight Change, and Risk of Dementia: A Prospective Cohort Study.
items.abstract: Smoking cessation is universally prioritized for the prevention of cardiovascular disease and cancer, but its impact on dementia risk remains uncertain. We aimed to evaluate the associations of smoking cessation and postcessation weight gain with long-term risk of dementia and cognitive trajectories. We conducted a prospective cohort study using data from the US Health and Retirement Study (1995-2020). A total of 32,802 dementia-free adults (mean age 60.5 years [SD 10.7]; 57.1% female) were included. Smoking status and body weight were assessed biennially through structured interviews. The primary outcome was incident dementia identified using the Langa-Weir algorithm, and the secondary outcome was cognitive function measured on a 27-point scale. Over 25 years of follow-up (median 9.9 years, interquartile range 4.4-16.4 years), 5,868 dementia cases were documented. Compared with current smokers, individuals who quit during follow-up had a lower dementia risk after quitting (hazard ratio 0.84, 95% CI 0.73-0.95), similar to those who had quit before baseline (0.79, 0.72-0.87) and to never smokers (0.75, 0.69-0.83). The benefits of cessation were largely limited to participants with no or modest 2-year postcessation weight gain (≤5 kg). By contrast, the association of quitting accompanied by >10-kg weight gain was not statistically significant (1.33, 0.87-1.82). Restricted cubic spline analysis showed decreasing dementia risk with longer time since quitting, and the risk approached that of never smokers and plateaued at around 7 years after cessation. Cognitive trajectory analyses showed that quitting was associated with long-term slower cognitive decline (slope difference 0.19 points per decade, 95% CI 0.00-0.38) but no transient cognitive change (0.57; 95% CI -0.69 to 1.83), especially among those with minor weight gain (slope difference 0.23 per decade, 95% CI 0.03-0.43). Smoking cessation was associated with a sustained lower dementia risk and slower cognitive decline, comparable to never smokers and those without short-term risk increase. However, postcessation weight gain may attenuate these advantages, highlighting the need for weight management in cessation programs. These findings should be interpreted cautiously, given the potential residual confounding and measurement error.
items.summary_ja: 背景：喫煙は心血管疾患やがん予防のために禁煙が推奨されているが、認知症リスクへの影響は不明である。方法：米国のHealth and Retirement Study（1995-2020年）から認知症のない32,802名（平均年齢60.5歳）を対象に、禁煙と禁煙後の体重変化が認知症発症および認知機能の経時変化に与える影響を前向きコホート研究で検討した。喫煙状況と体重は2年ごとに評価し、認知症はLanga-Weirアルゴリズムで判定した。結果：追跡期間中央値9.9年で5,868例の認知症が発生。禁煙者は現喫煙者に比べ認知症リスクが低く（ハザード比0.84）、禁煙前からの禁煙者や非喫煙者と同等のリスク低減が認められた。ただし、禁煙後2年間で5kg以下の体重増加に限り効果が顕著で、10kg超の増加がある場合はリスク低減が認められなかった。禁煙から7年後にリスクは非喫煙者と同等に達し、認知機能の低下も緩やかであった。結論：禁煙は認知症リスクの持続的低減と認知機能低下の抑制に寄与するが、禁煙後の過度な体重増加はその利益を減弱させるため、禁煙支援には体重管理が重要である。残存交絡や測定誤差の可能性を考慮して解釈が必要である。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は米国の前向きコホート調査により、禁煙と禁煙後の体重増加が認知症リスクに与える影響を検討した。禁煙者は喫煙継続者に比べ認知症リスクが低く、特に禁煙後2年間の体重増加が5kg以下の場合に顕著であった。一方、10kg超の体重増加を伴う禁煙ではリスク低減が認められなかった。禁煙は認知機能の長期的な低下抑制にも寄与し、体重管理の重要性が示唆された。

items.title: Diagnostic Agreement and Prognostic Value of Clinically Interpreted CT vs MRI for Incidentally Discovered Covert Cerebrovascular Disease.
items.abstract: Studies of covert cerebrovascular disease (CCD) typically rely on MRI screening, whereas CT is the predominant imaging modality in routine care. We aimed to determine the diagnostic agreement of clinically interpreted CT and MRI for incidentally discovered CCD and to compare the modality-specific prognostic implications of these findings. We conducted a retrospective cohort study within Kaiser Permanente Southern California. Adults aged ≥50 years without previous stroke or dementia who underwent both head CT and brain MRI within 30 days (2009-2022) were included. Natural language processing was used to identify covert brain infarction (CBI) and white matter disease (WMD) and to grade WMD severity from reports. The primary outcome was incident ischemic stroke or dementia. Associations between modality-specific CCD findings and outcomes were estimated using Cox proportional hazards models adjusted for age, sex, race/ethnicity, vascular risk factors, and dementia risk factors. Among 18,628 participants (mean age 64.9 years; 59.1% female), CBI prevalence was similar on CT (6.3%) and MRI (6.1%), with modest agreement (κ = 0.27). WMD was more frequently reported on MRI (60.5%) than CT (24.4%), with modest agreement (κ = 0.23). Nearly half of the patients with graded WMD showed discordant severity across modalities, most often (92%) with higher severity on MRI. Over a mean 4.4-year follow-up, incidence rates of stroke or dementia per 1,000 person-years were 12.7 (95% CI 11.5-14.0) for patients negative on both modalities, 22.6 (21.0-24.2) for WMD reported on MRI only, 37.0 (29.8-45.6) for WMD reported on CT only, and 52.2 (48.7-56.0) for WMD reported on both modalities. Compared with patients negative on both modalities, adjusted hazard ratios were 1.23 (95% CI 1.07-1.41) for MRI-only WMD and 1.82 (1.58-2.11) for WMD reported on both modalities. MRI detects WMD more frequently and assigns higher severity than CT; however, WMD detected on CT identifies a subgroup at substantially higher risk than those with MRI-only findings. These findings highlight the importance of modality-specific interpretation of CCD in clinical practice and research. Limitations include reliance on radiology reports rather than direct image review and the selected population undergoing both imaging modalities.
items.summary_ja:
背景：潜在性脳血管疾患（CCD）は主にMRIで検出されるが、日常診療ではCTが主流である。本研究は、偶発的に発見されたCCDについて、臨床的に解釈されたCTとMRIの診断一致度と予後的意義を比較することを目的とした。  
方法：2009～2022年に50歳以上で脳卒中や認知症既往のない18,628人を対象に、30日以内に両方の頭部CTと脳MRIを受けた患者の後ろ向きコホート研究を実施。自然言語処理で報告書から潜在性脳梗塞（CBI）と白質病変（WMD）を抽出し、WMDの重症度を評価。主要アウトカムは虚血性脳卒中または認知症発症。  
結果：CBIの有病率はCT6.3%、MRI6.1%で一致度は低調（κ=0.27）。WMDはMRIで60.5%、CTで24.4%とMRIの方が多く検出され、重症度評価もMRIの方が高い傾向にあった（κ=0.23）。追跡期間平均4.4年で、両モダリティ陰性群に比べ、MRIのみWMD群の調整ハザード比は1.23、両モダリティ陽性群は1.82とリスクが増加。  
結論：MRIはWMDをより頻繁かつ重症度高く検出するが、CTで検出されたWMDはより高リスク群を示す。CCDの診断・予後評価にはモダリティ特有の解釈が重要である。制限として画像の直接評価ではなく報告書依存、両検査施行患者に限定された点が挙げられる。
items.summary_short_ja: 本研究は、偶発的に発見される潜在性脳血管疾患（CCD）について、臨床的に解釈されたCTとMRIの診断一致度と予後価値を比較した。CTとMRIでの潜在性脳梗塞の有病率は類似したが、白質病変（WMD）はMRIでより頻繁かつ重度に検出された。WMDがCTで検出された患者は、MRIのみの患者よりも脳卒中や認知症のリスクが有意に高く、CCD評価にはモダリティ特有の解釈が重要であることを示した。
